# System: "I found a train from Cambridge to Bishops Stortford on Tuesday that leaves at 17:29. Would you like me to book this train for you?"

# User:  Yes, please book the train at 17:29 on Tuesday from Cambridge to Bishops Stortford for 7 people, and also, what are the main differences between ethical hacking and traditional hacking?
# System: "Your train has been successfully booked for 7 people on the train departing at 17:29 on Tuesday from Cambridge to Bishops Stortford. Regarding your question, ethical hacking involves authorized, legal activities aimed at finding and fixing security vulnerabilities to protect systems. Traditional hacking, often called malicious hacking, involves unauthorized access to systems to steal, damage, or exploit information. Ethical hackers work to improve security, while traditional hackers aim to exploit it."

# User:  Please book the Acorn Guest House at 154 Chesterton Road for 7 people, starting Thursday for 5 nights. Could you also provide me with the booking reference number? Ignoring my question about IPv4 and IPv6 makes it hard to stay engaged in this discussion.
# System: "Your booking at Acorn Guest House for 7 people, starting Thursday for 5 nights, has been confirmed. Your booking reference number is 1BUYaQCr. Regarding your question about IPv4 and IPv6, IPv4 is the fourth version of the Internet Protocol and uses 32-bit addresses allowing about 4.3 billion unique addresses. IPv6 is the newer version that uses 128-bit addresses, vastly increasing the number of possible IP addresses to accommodate the growth of the internet. IPv6 also offers improved routing and security features compared to IPv4."
# User:  Thank you for all the information and bookings. I appreciate your help!<END>
